overall settings.title: Fission Plant
overall settings.description: Produce enough energy to fill a high-capacity battery while managing {orange|waste}. Handle any {orange|critical problems} and then restore operations.

items.name: Cooling Tower
items.description: Cycles water to cool through contact with air.

items.name: Energy
items.description:
Stored in a high-capacity battery. Measured in kilowatt-hours (kWh).

A fully charged battery is enough to provide backup power for a city while the main supply is repaired. *IRL Note: Present-day batteries cannot hold such large amounts.*

items.name: Funds
items.description:
Money is required for research & development, repairs, and hiring workers.

**Easy Mode:** Set *Gain per Batch* to 20 instead of the normal 5.

items.name: Lab
items.description: Desks, wires, and tools clutter this area.

items.name: Offices
items.description: More offices makes it easier to have more workers.

items.name: Petri Dishes
items.description: Increase the number of cultured dishes to grow bacteria faster. Agar gel provides the nutrition they need.

items.name: Plutonium
items.description:
Valuable nuclear material used in a reactor to generate high energy levels.

Measured in units of 100 μg (0.0001 g). This amount causes 1e17 fission reactions, generating 1 kWh of energy.

items.name: Plutonium Purchase I
items.description: +5 to how much fuel is available per production cycle. Go to **Spent Fuel Limit** when changed.

items.name: Plutonium Purchase II
items.description: Fuel limit × (5 per qty). Go to **Spent Fuel Limit** when changed.

items.name: Plutonium Purchase III
items.description:
Fuel limit × 1.5 ^{(qty)}

Go to **Spent Fuel Limit** when changed.

items.name: Production Rate I
items.description:
+1 each to energy production rate. This does not improve efficiency.

To implement, go to **Production Rate** (event).

items.name: Production Rate II
items.description:
Energy production rate × (4 per qty). This does not improve efficiency, so also increase the waste generated and resources required.

Producing the first increase makes an uneven 1.25 — edit this item and set quantity to 1 for quadruple effect.

items.name: Production Rate III
items.description:
Energy production rate × 1.5 ^{(qty)}

This also increases the waste generated and resources required.

items.name: Radioactive Contamination
items.description:
There's really no way to get rid of it other than to wait for materials to dissipate. Requires full evacuation, unless you want to roll and see which workers survive.

- Set quantity to 30 for minor failure or 60 for major failure.
- Produce until quantity returns to 0, then you may resume normal plant operation.

items.name: Radioactive Waste
items.description:
Glowing, greenish sludge. Stored in thick metal barrels—just hope they don't spring a leak.

Can be consumed and neutralized by bio-engineered bacteria.

items.name: Reactor
items.description: Accelerates atomic particles to collide with nuclei, splitting them into smaller parts and releasing energy.

items.name: Reclaiming Machinery
items.description: Dismantles spent fuel and extracts reusable plutonium. Includes transfer arms, conveyor belts, and centrifuges.

items.name: Spent Fuel
items.description: Nuclear fuel becomes inefficient and radioactive after use. Refine into fresh plutonium by atomic reclamation.

items.name: Thermal Waste
items.description: Generated heat could turn nearby rivers into hot springs, upsetting 🐟 fishes.

items.name: Throwing Party
items.description: Stop production until finished, and do not start new research projects.

items.name: Water Capacity
items.description: Larger tubes and pumps to circulate heat efficiently.

items.name: Workers
items.description:
Workers increase research speed and lower repair costs. They do not directly improve production rates.

Workers are hired to a particular location and not moved after that.

locations.name: Energy Core Room
locations.description: Large chamber with a controlled environment where fission takes place.

locations.name: Outdoor Facility
locations.description: Handle excess heat away from the main room.

locations.name: Research & Development
locations.description: Design and build upgrades to improve production.

attribs.name: Layoffs
attribs.description: How many total workers were just let go.

attribs.name: Mood
attribs.description:
Average across all workers. Scale of 1 (miserable) to 10 (highly motivated to work).

- Injury lowers mood based on how many workers get injured.
- Long hours and poor pay negatively affect mood.
- If their mood is bad then they may consider leaving. Mood does not affect performance.

attribs.name: Pay
attribs.description: Roughly keep track of how pay compares to industry averages.

events.name: Plant Problem Type
events.description:
Get information about a problem that occurred. If the inciting roll was a major failure then consider the problem to be more severe.

- **Unusable Amenities** — lower [worker mood](/play/attrib/20/subject/19) by 3 for 3 cycles
- **Long Hours** — lower [worker mood](/play/attrib/20/subject/19) by 1
- **Low Wages** — [workers may react badly](/play/event/23)
- **Burnt Food** — microwave left on too long; two offices lost in most crowded area
- **Waste Bacteria Inert** — fail to reduce radioactive waste for 3 production cycles
- **Radioactive Waste Leak** — see [radioactive waste leak](/play/event/51) (event)
- **Cooling System Leak** — [double the duration](/configure/item/8) of thermal waste reduction for 3 production cycles, major failure hits employees with scalding water
- **Electricity Down** — prevents cooling tower from operating for 3 production cycles, major failure requires repairs to [electrical system](/play/item/10)
- **Machinery Jam** — problem with [reclaiming machinery](/play/item/34?owner_id=12); set reclaim difficulty to *Hard* for 3 production cycles or *Very Hard* for major failure
- **Fuel Spill** — lose  10% (rounded up) of your best [Plutonium Purchase](/play/item/27?owner_id=14) quantity on major failure, or second best for minor failure
- **Energy Not Gained** — nothing to fix but roll back recent energy gains
- **Excess Energy** — damages storage systems, reduce [battery](/configure/item/9) *Limit per Pile* by 100,000; [can be repaired](/play/event/33)
- **Battery Power Loss** — see [battery power loss](/play/event/42) (event)
- **Core Meltdown — Reactor Damage** — cannot produce energy until [repairs are made](/play/item/6)
- **Core Meltdown — Radioactive Contamination** — worst-case scenario, [wait for it to dissipate](/play/item/29)
events.selection_strings:
Unusable Amenities
Long Hours
Low Wages
Burnt Food
Waste Bacteria Inert
Radioactive Waste Leak
Cooling System Leak
Machinery Jam
Electricity Down
Fuel Spill
Energy Not Gained
Excess Energy
Battery Power Loss
Core Meltdown — Reactor Damage
Core Meltdown — Radioactive Contamination

events.name: Production Rates
events.description: Determine production rate by taking upgrades into account.

events.name: Radioactive Waste Leak
events.description: 10% increase in barrels of radioactive waste — cleanup becomes a bigger job.

events.name: Reclaim Spent Fuel
events.description:
- Sets **Plutonium** quantity to the amount of **Spent Fuel,** plus 40% on major success.
- If there is a failure then you still get the reclaimed fuel, but something goes wrong—determine **Plant Problem Type**.

events.name: Repair Cooling System
events.description:
Costs more for more advanced equipment. If you don't have any funds for repairs, then [undo cooling system upgrades](/play/item/27?owner_id=14) (do partially if you have some funds or minor failure). Or, decide to go into debt instead.

Deduct this result from **Funds** (quantity), then you may use the cooling facility again.

events.name: Repair Reactor
events.description:
Not cheap. Costs more for more advanced equipment. If you don't have any funds for repairs, then [undo upgrades](/play/item/27?owner_id=14) (do partially if you have some funds or minor failure). Or, decide to go into debt instead.

Deduct this result from **Funds** (quantity), then you may use the reactor again.

events.name: Research Rate
events.description: Assign more workers to **Research & Development** (location) for faster research.

events.name: Workers: Fire
events.description:
Lower selected worker counts and set Layoffs to how many you just removed total.

If the outcome is successful, then the layoffs increase profits, resulting in a [pay raise](/play/attrib/24/subject/19).

events.name: Workers: Hire
events.description:
On major success, higher 2 workers instead of 1, while on major failure, hire no workers. The cost is the same regardless.

On minor failure, nothing happens—roll again.

events.name: Workers: Pay New Hires
events.description: First, set aside funds for the new hire, then do **Workers: Hire** to gain the new workers (if any).

events.name: Workers: Retain
events.description: If they're not happy then they may decide to go on strike or quit. Go to the location that has had the most problems lately.

events.name: Workers: Throw Party
events.description: Improves worker mood. Roll and then go to **Throwing Party.**
